message.content:
Under the **Understandable** principle, WCAG 2.2 has **17 success criteria** across Guidelines 3.1, 3.2, and 3.3.[5][6] The list below includes the four **new in WCAG 2.2** criteria you named: **3.2.6, 3.3.7, 3.3.8, and 3.3.9**.[2][5]

| SC number | Official name | Level | What it verifies | axe-core vs. manual |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| 3.1.1 | Language of Page | A | The default natural language of the page is programmatically declared. | **Manual + some automated** |
| 3.1.2 | Language of Parts | AA | Changes in language within content are programmatically identified. | **Manual + some automated** |
| 3.1.3 | Unusual Words | AAA | Unusual, jargon, or specialized words are explained. | **Manual** |
| 3.1.4 | Abbreviations | AAA | Abbreviations are expanded or explained the first time they appear. | **Manual** |
| 3.1.5 | Reading Level | AAA | Content is written so that a lower secondary education reading level is not required, or a version is available. | **Manual** |
| 3.1.6 | Pronunciation | AAA | Pronunciation of words that are ambiguous in context is provided when needed to understand them. | **Manual** |
| 3.2.1 | On Focus | A | Moving focus to an element does not trigger an unexpected change of context. | **Manual + some automated** |
| 3.2.2 | On Input | A | Changing input values does not trigger an unexpected change of context unless the user is warned first. | **Manual + some automated** |
| 3.2.3 | Consistent Navigation | AA | Repeated navigation components appear in a consistent relative order. | **Manual + some automated** |
| 3.2.4 | Consistent Identification | AA | Components with the same function are identified consistently. | **Manual + some automated** |
| 3.2.5 | Change on Request | AAA | Context changes initiated by user input happen only when requested or confirmed. | **Manual** |
| 3.2.6 | Consistent Help | A | Help mechanisms are placed in a consistent relative order across pages when they are repeated. | **Manual** |
| 3.3.1 | Error Identification | A | Input errors are identified and described in text. | **Manual + some automated** |
| 3.3.2 | Labels or Instructions | A | Form fields and controls have clear labels/instructions. | **Manual + some automated** |
| 3.3.3 | Error Suggestion | AA | When possible, errors are explained with suggested corrections. | **Manual** |
| 3.3.4 | Error Prevention (Legal, Financial, Data) | AA | Critical submissions can be reversed, checked, or confirmed before finalizing. | **Manual** |
| 3.3.5 | Help | AAA | Context-sensitive help is available for user input when needed. | **Manual** |
| 3.3.6 | Error Prevention (All) | AAA | All submissions that cause legal/financial/data changes can be reversed, checked, or confirmed. | **Manual** |
| 3.3.7 | Redundant Entry | A | Previously entered information is not required again in the same process unless exceptions apply. | **Manual** |
| 3.3.8 | Accessible Authentication (Minimum) | AA | Authentication does not depend solely on a cognitive function test unless exceptions apply. | **Manual** |
| 3.3.9 | Accessible Authentication (Enhanced) | AAA | Authentication avoids cognitive function tests and also disallows certain recognition-based authentication methods. | **Manual** |

A few notes on the automation column:

- **axe-core** generally catches only parts of these SCs where the requirement is machine-detectable in the DOM or accessibility tree; most of the Understandable criteria still require **manual review** to judge intent, behavior, or content quality.
- For the items in this section, **manual testing is always required** for full conformance assessment, even when tools can help with partial signals.
- The four WCAG 2.2 additions in this principle are **3.2.6 Consistent Help**, **3.3.7 Redundant Entry**, **3.3.8 Accessible Authentication (Minimum)**, and **3.3.9 Accessible Authentication (Enhanced)**.[2][5]

If you want, I can also provide this as a **CSV-style table** or add a **tooling notes column** with the specific kinds of checks axe-core can and cannot detect for each criterion.
